Name: National Clinical Trials Governance Framework
Description: National framework for clinical trial governance and ethics oversight.

Name: Privacy Act 1988
Description: Australian data protection law governing personal information handling.

Name: Therapeutic Goods Act 1989
Description: Core Australian law regulating therapeutic goods including medicines and devices.

Name: Therapeutic Goods Administration Software-based Medical Device Guidance
Description: Regulatory guidance for software and AI medical devices.

Name: ANVISA Resolution RDC No. 751/2022
Description: Regulation defining requirements for software as a medical device.

Name: Brazil General Data Protection Law
Description: Brazilian data protection law governing personal data processing.

Name: Law No. 6,360/1976 (Brazil Health Surveillance Law)
Description: Brazilian health surveillance law governing medicines and pharmaceuticals.

Name: Canada Vigilance Program
Description: National adverse drug reaction reporting and pharmacovigilance system.

Name: Food and Drug Regulations (Canada)
Description: Regulations under Food and Drugs Act governing drug approval.

Name: Food and Drugs Act (Canada)
Description: Core Canadian law regulating food and drugs including pharmaceuticals.

Name: Medical Devices Regulations (Canada)
Description: Regulations governing medical devices including software-based devices.

Name: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act
Description: Canadian data protection law for commercial personal information.

Name: Drug Administration Law of the People's Republic of China
Description: Primary Chinese drug law governing lifecycle regulation of medicinal products including clinical trials, manufacturing, approval, and post-market surveillance under the NMPA.

Name: Personal Information Protection Law of the People's Republic of China
Description: Comprehensive Chinese data protection law regulating processing of personal and sensitive data, including health and genetic information. Requires lawful basis, consent, security controls, and strict rules for cross-border data transfer.

Name: Artificial Intelligence Act (EU Regulation 2024/1689)
Description: EU legal framework for AI systems using a risk-based classification approach. Imposes obligations on high-risk AI including governance, transparency, data quality, human oversight, and post-market monitoring.

Name: Clinical Trials Regulation (EU No 536/2014)
Description: EU regulation harmonizing clinical trial authorization, conduct, and reporting via a centralized submission system (CTIS). Improves transparency, safety reporting, and consistency across member states.

Name: DARWIN EU (Data Analysis and Real World Interrogation Network)
Description: EU real-world data network enabling federated access to healthcare datasets for regulatory research. Supports evidence generation for pharmacovigilance, safety monitoring, and effectiveness studies using harmonized data sources.

Name: EU AI Act Governance and Enforcement Framework
Description: Institutional enforcement structure for the EU AI Act, defining roles of the EU AI Office and national competent authorities responsible for supervision, conformity assessment, and market enforcement.

Name: EU GMP Annex 11: Computerised Systems
Description: EU GMP guidance defining validation, audit trail, access control, and lifecycle requirements for computerized systems used in GMP environments, including software and AI integrated into regulated processes.

Name: EU GMP Annex 22: Artificial Intelligence (Draft/Guidance)
LifecycleDomain: Manufacturing (mapped to Regulatory Review phase per your allowed schema boundary)
Description: Draft/emerging GMP guidance addressing AI/ML use in manufacturing. Focuses on validation, transparency, human oversight, and restrictions on autonomous decision-making in critical GMP processes.

Name: European Health Data Space Regulation
Description: EU framework enabling secure primary and secondary use of health data for healthcare delivery and research. Establishes governance for data access, interoperability, and controlled use in research and innovation.

Name: Falsified Medicines Directive (EU Directive 2011/62/EU)
Description: EU regulation introducing serialization and anti-tampering requirements for medicines. Enables verification across the supply chain to prevent counterfeit products and improve traceability.

Name: General Data Protection Regulation (EU Regulation 2016/679)
Description: EU data protection regulation governing processing of personal data, including health data. Requires lawful basis, consent, minimization, pseudonymization, and cross-border transfer controls.

Name: Good Pharmacovigilance Practices (EU GVP Modules)
Description: EU pharmacovigilance framework defining requirements for safety monitoring, including adverse event reporting, signal detection, risk management plans, and periodic safety reporting.

Name: Network and Information Security Directive 2 (EU Directive 2022/2555)
Description: EU cybersecurity directive expanding obligations for critical infrastructure, including healthcare and pharma. Requires incident reporting, risk management, and supply chain cybersecurity controls.

Name: Regulation (EC) No 726/2004
Description: EU regulation establishing centralized authorization procedure for medicinal products via EMA, including post-authorization safety oversight and regulatory coordination mechanisms.

Name: EMA/FDA Guiding principles of good AI practice in drug development (EMA/FDA)
Description: Joint FDA–EMA principles outlining shared expectations for responsible AI in drug development, including governance, transparency, risk management, and lifecycle monitoring.

Name: Good Manufacturing Practice (EU GMP + US 21 CFR 210/211)
Description: Regulatory framework governing pharmaceutical manufacturing quality systems, including process validation, batch control, documentation, and inspection readiness.

Name: G7 Code of Conduct for Advanced AI Systems
Description: Voluntary international framework for safe and responsible development of advanced AI systems, emphasizing transparency, security, and risk mitigation.

Name: AI Data Quality Standards
Description: Data quality standards for AI and machine learning systems, covering dataset quality, documentation, and bias mitigation.

Name: AI Governance Standard
Description: AI governance standard defining organizational and board-level responsibilities for AI oversight and accountability.

Name: AI Management System Standard
Description: First international AI management system standard defining governance, lifecycle controls, accountability, and auditability for AI systems.

Name: AI Risk Management Framework (NIST)
Description: Risk management framework for AI systems defining governance functions across mapping, measuring, managing, and governing AI risks.

Name: AI Risk Management Standard
Description: AI risk management standard providing structured processes for identifying, assessing, and mitigating AI-specific risks.

Name: AI System Lifecycle Standard
Description: AI system lifecycle standard defining processes for development, deployment, monitoring, and maintenance of AI systems.

Name: ALCOA+ Data Integrity Principles
Description: Core data integrity framework requiring data to be attributable, legible, contemporaneous, original, and accurate (ALCOA), extended with completeness, consistency, durability, and availability. Applied across GLP, GCP, GMP, and pharmacovigilance systems; foundational expectation for audit trails and electronic data integrity in regulated environments.

Name: Business Continuity Management Systems
Description: Business continuity management standard ensuring resilience of critical operations, including pharma manufacturing and pharmacovigilance systems.

Name: Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium Standards (SDTM / ADaM / Define-XML)
Description: Clinical data standards defining structured formats for regulatory submission datasets. Enables standardized analysis (ADaM) and tabulation (SDTM) for FDA/EMA/PMDA submissions. Supports reproducibility and interoperability of clinical trial data, including AI-processed datasets.

Name: Good Automated Manufacturing Practice 5
Description: Industry validation framework for computerized systems in regulated environments. Defines lifecycle validation approach (URS, IQ, OQ, PQ) for GxP software including AI systems.

Name: Good Clinical Practice (ICH E6)
Description: International ethical and scientific standard for clinical trials, ensuring protection of human subjects, data integrity, and clear sponsor/investigator responsibilities.

Name: Good Distribution Practice
Description: GDP defines requirements for pharmaceutical distribution, including traceability, temperature control, storage conditions, and prevention of counterfeit or damaged products entering supply chains.

Name: Health Level Seven Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources
Description: Healthcare interoperability standard defining structured formats and APIs for exchanging electronic health records, enabling integration across clinical, research, and AI systems.

Name: Health Software Product Safety Standard
Description: Standard for health software product safety and quality, particularly for standalone digital health applications and AI-based software.

Name: Industrial Cybersecurity Standard (ISA/IEC 62443)
Description: Industrial cybersecurity standard for protecting operational technology (OT) systems, including manufacturing and pharmaceutical production environments.

Name: Information Security Management Systems + Cybersecurity Framework
Description: Combined cybersecurity framework: ISO 27001 defines information security management systems; NIST CSF provides operational cybersecurity functions (identify, protect, detect, respond, recover).

Name: International Council for Harmonisation Guidelines (Q/S/E/M Series)
Description: Harmonized international regulatory framework covering quality (Q), safety (S), efficacy (E), and multidisciplinary (M) requirements for drug development and submission.

Name: Medical Device Risk Management Standard
Description: Risk management standard for medical devices and health software, defining hazard identification, risk evaluation, and mitigation processes.

Name: Medical Device Software Lifecycle Standard
Description: Standard defining lifecycle processes for medical software development, maintenance, and risk classification, widely used for regulated clinical software and AI systems.

Name: Medical Devices Quality Management Systems
Description: Quality management system standard for medical devices, defining design controls, risk management, and regulatory compliance processes for device/software development.

Name: OECD Principles on Artificial Intelligence
Description: International principles for trustworthy AI including transparency, robustness, accountability, and human-centered design.

Name: Software as a Medical Device Framework (IMDRF/FDA aligned)
Description: International framework defining classification and regulatory pathways for software as a medical device (SaMD), based on risk and clinical impact.

Name: Storage Security Standard
Description: Standard for secure data storage systems, covering protection of sensitive datasets such as clinical, genomic, and real-world evidence data.

Name: WHO Ethics and Governance of AI for Health
Description: WHO principles for responsible AI in health systems focusing on transparency, inclusiveness, and patient safety.

Name: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission
Description: National digital health infrastructure enabling interoperable health records.

Name: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (India, 2023)
Description: India’s modern data protection law governing digital personal data processing.

Name: Drugs and Cosmetics Act (India, 1940)
Description: Primary Indian drug law regulating manufacture, sale, and distribution of drugs.

Name: Medical Device Rules (India, 2017)
Description: Regulation covering medical devices including software-based devices.

Name: New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules (India, 2019)
Description: Regulation governing clinical trials and new drug approvals in India.

Name: Act on the Protection of Personal Information (Japan)
Description: Data protection law governing personal data processing in Japan.

Name: Good Clinical Practice (Japan)
Description: Clinical trial conduct standards aligned with ICH E6.

Name: Good Vigilance Practice / Good Post-Marketing Study Practice (Japan)
Description: Post-marketing safety and surveillance obligations for pharmaceuticals.

Name: Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Act (Japan)
Description: Core Japanese law governing pharmaceuticals, medical devices, and SaMD regulation.

Name: SAKIGAKE Designation System
Description: Accelerated approval pathway for innovative drugs and digital health technologies.

Name: SaMD / Digital Health Guidance (PMDA)
Description: Regulatory guidance for software as a medical device including AI-based systems.

Name: Health Products Act (Singapore)
Description: Singapore law regulating health products including medicines and devices.

Name: HSA SaMD Guidance GN-23 / GN-27
Description: Regulatory guidance for software as a medical device.

Name: Model AI Governance Framework (Singapore IMDA)
Description: National framework for responsible AI development and deployment.

Name: Personal Data Protection Act (Singapore)
Description: Data protection law governing personal data in Singapore.

Name: Medical Device Software / AI Guidance (MFDS Korea)
Description: Regulatory guidance for AI-based medical devices.

Name: Personal Information Protection Act (South Korea)
Description: Korean data protection law governing personal information processing.

Name: Pharmaceutical Affairs Act (South Korea)
Description: Core Korean pharmaceutical regulation framework.

Name: Federal Act on Data Protection (Switzerland)
Description: Swiss data protection law governing personal data processing.

Name: Swissmedic Medical Device Software Guidance
Description: Guidance on classification and requirements for medical device software.

Name: Therapeutic Products Act (Switzerland)
Description: Swiss law regulating therapeutic products including drugs and devices.

Name: AI Airlock Programme (MHRA UK)
Description: Regulatory sandbox enabling real-world evaluation of AI medical devices under MHRA supervision.

Name: Data Protection Act 2018 (UK)
Description: UK implementing legislation complementing UK GDPR.

Name: Human Medicines Regulations 2012
Description: UK statutory framework governing authorization, manufacture, and distribution of medicines.

Name: Human Medicines Regulations 2012 (Clinical Trials provisions)
Description: Regulatory framework governing clinical trial authorization and ethics approval in the UK.

Name: MHRA SaMD / AI Guidance
Description: UK regulatory guidance defining classification and oversight expectations for software and AI used in medical and clinical contexts.

Name: NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit
Description: Self-assessment framework for data security compliance in NHS data environments.

Name: Software and AI as a Medical Device Guidance (MHRA)
Description: UK guidance defining classification and regulatory expectations for AI and software used as medical devices.

Name: UK General Data Protection Regulation
Description: Data protection law governing processing of personal and health data in the UK.

Name: Cybersecurity Guidance for Medical Devices
Description: FDA guidance requiring cybersecurity controls for medical devices and connected systems, including secure design, vulnerability management, and Software Bill of Materials (SBOM).

Name: Data Integrity Guidance for Industry
Description: US FDA guidance defining expectations for trustworthy electronic data in GxP systems, including audit trails, metadata integrity, and prevention of data falsification.

Name: Drug Supply Chain Security Act
Description: US law establishing electronic, interoperable traceability of prescription drugs at package level. Enables verification, serialization, suspect product investigation, and recall management across the pharmaceutical supply chain.

Name: FDA 21 CFR Part 11
Description: US regulation defining requirements for electronic records and signatures in regulated environments. Requires audit trails, system validation, access controls, and data integrity safeguards for GxP systems.

Name: Good Laboratory Practice
Description: US regulation governing non-clinical laboratory studies supporting drug development, requiring SOPs, QA oversight, and data traceability for toxicology and safety studies.

Name: Good Machine Learning Practice
Description: FDA principles for developing safe and effective machine learning systems in medical contexts, emphasizing dataset representativeness, validation, monitoring, and clinical safety.

Name: Investigational New Drug Application Regulations (21 CFR Part 312)
Description: US regulation governing Investigational New Drug applications for human clinical trials, including preclinical evidence requirements, protocol submission, and regulatory authorization before first-in-human studies.

Name: NDA/BLA Regulations
Description: US regulatory framework for New Drug Applications and Biologics License Applications. Defines requirements for safety, efficacy, manufacturing controls (CMC), labeling, and post-market obligations.

Name: Real-World Evidence Program
Description: FDA framework enabling use of real-world data for regulatory decisions such as label expansions and safety evaluations. Defines expectations for data quality and analytical validation.

Name: Sentinel Initiative
Description: US active surveillance system using distributed healthcare data networks to monitor post-market drug safety and detect adverse events in near real time.
